summary: Spolsky dissects the management consulting industry's approach to measuring software developer productivity, arguing it's fundamentally fraudulent. He walks through a step-by-step scenario where consultants sell bogus productivity metrics to large companies, programmers game those metrics, and consultants then use the inflated numbers to sell more engagements to competitors. He argues that knowledge work productivity metrics are trivially gameable, making the entire exercise a pointless wealth transfer from corporations to consulting firms. The kicker is that the consultants themselves don't even realize the fraud they're perpetuating.
key_points: Software development productivity cannot be meaningfully measured with metrics like lines of code or function points
Programmers will inevitably game any productivity metric imposed on them, such as inflating function point counts through unnecessary XML round-trips
The consulting industry creates a self-perpetuating cycle by using one client's gamed metrics to scare competitors into buying the same services
Performance metrics in knowledge organizations are completely trivial to game, making consultant-driven productivity improvement an illusion
The consultants themselves are often unaware they're participating in a fraud because they lack the technical knowledge to see through the gaming
money_quotes: The whole fraud is only possible because performance metrics in knowledge organizations are completely trivial to game.
I think it was something about measuring performance in software organizations? aHA! YES! I know all about that. You can't do that. Quit it. Stop it.

You can triple the number of function points in your code simply by round tripping everything through an XML file. Big waste of time, prone to bugs, does nothing, but each file you touch adds a function point.
All they've done is caused a fairly pointless transfer of wealth from ExxonMobilConoco to BainMcKinseyGartner's senior partners.
The stunningly good-looking, bright, earnest chipmunks with 4.0s in Russian Lit from Harvard who work for these companies, have absolutely no way of knowing this.
They get all the way through the 2-year associate program on their way to MBA school without even realizing that they haven't done a goddamn thing about productivity.
key_insight: Software productivity metrics are so easily gamed by developers that any attempt to measure and optimize them through external consulting becomes a self-perpetuating fraud that enriches consultants while accomplishing nothing.
